proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=97, 93.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +42.3pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8690 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=97, wr=93.7%, edge +42.3pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=97, Wilson lower 0.869, +42.3pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61 below the Tier S threshold. Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (shifting, day 4), so it is dormant; portfolio Bear exposure is 0/29 and no regime concentration is triggered (0.5517 below 0.80), so promotion does not compound risk. Consistent with continuous prior approvals (2026-04-29 through 2026-07-22) and no trader rejection on record.

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=BULL_PROXY, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +34.4pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.6566 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.98 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
approval would propagate redundant to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +34.4pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification is robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 above Tier M floor, +34.4pp edge over BULL_PROXY baseline of 53.85%) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.98 (<1.0). Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (shifting, day 4), so it is dormant; portfolio Bear exposure is 0/29 and no regime concentration is triggered (0.5517 below 0.80), so promotion does not compound concentration risk and may diversify when Bear re-emerges. Consistent with continuous prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-06-19 through 07-22) with no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_signal_type = UP_TRI (100.0%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_signal_type = UP_TRI; recommended action: Pause new UP_TRI entries until either (a) signal_type mix drops below 85% via natural Day-6 exits or via addition of a non-UP_TRI signal type, or (b) Bull×UP_TRI or Choppy×UP_TRI cohort promotes to Tier M/S validating the directional edge.
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: A regime flip to Bear or a sharp Choppy-driven mean-reversion sweep would hit all 29 positions simultaneously with no offsetting short/neutral exposure; the Candidate-tier cohorts provide no statistical assurance that UP_TRI edge holds through such a transition, so drawdown would be book-wide and correlated.

evidence: UP_TRI = 29/29 = 100% of open positions (breaches 0.85 signal-type threshold)
Regime split Choppy 55.17% / Bull 44.83% — no validated regime edge
All 4 concentrated cells (Bull×IT, Bull×Metal, Choppy×Bank, Choppy×IT) at 10.34% share each map only to Candidate-tier cohorts
Regime concentration (0.5517) and per-cell shares (max 10.34%) are within tolerance — signal-type monoculture is the sole actionable breach
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new UP_TRI entries until either (a) signal_type mix drops below 85% via natural Day-6 exits or via addition of a non-UP_TRI signal type, or (b) Bull×UP_TRI or Choppy×UP_TRI cohort promotes to Tier M/S validating the directional edge.
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: A regime flip to Bear or a sharp Choppy-driven mean-reversion sweep would hit all 29 positions simultaneously with no offsetting short/neutral exposure; the Candidate-tier cohorts provide no statistical assurance that UP_TRI edge holds through such a transition, so drawdown would be book-wide and correlated.
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: UP_TRI is 100% of the book (29/29 = 1.0, well above the 0.85 signal-type threshold), making it a pure monoculture directional long. Regime is a Bull/Choppy mix (Choppy 55.17%, Bull 44.83%) — unstable, with no dominant regime to justify the one-sided posture. All four concentrated cells match only Candidate-tier cohorts (Bull×UP_TRI n=72, Choppy×UP_TRI n=302) — unvalidated edge across the entire book.
